mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness forces wage growth that boosts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and industrial production expansion signals robust aggregate demand, which lifts equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with the joint GDPC1-INDPRO growth composite, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the federal funds rate raise borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month when the tightening cycle is active.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral during consecutive 3-month FEDFUNDS hikes exceeding 0.50 percentage points, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals weakening consumer confidence and construction activity, which drags down corporate earnings expectations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of cases following a HOUST drop greater than 10 percent, or the signal shows no predictive power in a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding business activity and household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Sixty-day forward returns show no positive relationship with PAYEMS growth rates, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the consumer price index and the unemployment rate indicate stagflationary pressure, which erodes real corporate margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline when CPIAUCSL and UNRATE rise concurrently, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating industrial production growth signals supply-side bottlenecks resolving or demand fading, which historically precedes equity market corrections and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive when INDPRO growth decelerates for two consecutive months, or the signal's hit rate falls below 45 percent in a rolling 3-year window.
